title: Homers Night Out

jokes.text: Homer brags that a girl in Valve Maintenance 'has the hots for yours truly' while talking to his wife
jokes.setup: Homer describing office party drama to his wife
jokes.punchline: Homer's delusional claim that another woman wants him
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer obliviousness and ego - well-integrated character comedy that establishes his delusions

jokes.text: Just keeping you on your toes, babe.
jokes.setup: Marge's incredulous 'Homer' response to his bragging
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual dismissal as if he was being strategic
jokes.explanation: Quick character beat showing Homer's casual attitude toward his own nonsense

jokes.text: Two hundred and thirty nine pounds! Oh, I'm a blimp. Why are all the good things so tasty?
jokes.setup: Homer weighing himself
jokes.punchline: His philosophical lament about tasty food
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Homer - turning weight concerns into food appreciation. Relatable and character-specific

jokes.text: You're not a blimp, Homer. You're my big cuddly teddy bear.
jokes.setup: Homer calling himself a blimp
jokes.punchline: Marge's loving but still size-referencing reassurance
jokes.explanation: Sweet Marge moment that's still implicitly about his size - typical loving but inadvertently honest dynamic

jokes.text: Aw, baloney. Yeah, right.
jokes.setup: Marge's sweet teddy bear comment
jokes.punchline: Homer's dismissive reaction to his wife's kindness
jokes.explanation: Simple character beat showing Homer's self-awareness vs. self-pity

jokes.text: I'm a whale. Why was I cursed with this weakness for snack treats?
jokes.setup: Earlier called himself a blimp
jokes.punchline: Upgraded to whale, treats weakness as a curse
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from blimp to whale, plus Homer's dramatic self-victimization about snacks

jokes.text: Oh. Don't strain yourself, dear.
jokes.setup: Homer resolving to exercise every morning
jokes.punchline: Marge's dry, knowing response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge sarcasm - she knows Homer won't follow through. Economical and character-perfect

jokes.text: No, no, Marge. It's gonna be very classy. A tea-and-crumpets kind of thing.
jokes.setup: Marge asking if it's a stag party
jokes.punchline: Homer's absurd description of what's clearly going to be a stag party
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection - we know it won't be tea and crumpets. Homer's innocent deception is character-perfect

jokes.text: What is this, the Spanish Inquisition?
jokes.setup: Marge asking basic questions about his work event
jokes.punchline: Homer's dramatic overreaction to simple questions
jokes.explanation: Standard defensive husband joke, but well-delivered for Homer's character

jokes.text: Uh-oh, it's the 'fe-mailman.'
jokes.setup: Female mail carrier approaching
jokes.punchline: Homer's awkward portmanteau
jokes.explanation: Mild pun humor showing Homer's awkward awareness of gender-neutral language

jokes.text: Female carrier, Bart.
jokes.setup: Homer's awkward 'fe-mailman' comment
jokes.punchline: Bart's matter-of-fact correction
jokes.explanation: Great kid beat - Bart being more socially aware than his dad. Economical and character-smart

jokes.text: Every day for the last six months-- Where's my spy camera? Where's my spy camera?
jokes.setup: Mail carrier explaining Bart's daily harassment
jokes.punchline: Bart immediately proving her point by asking again
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure - complaint followed by immediate demonstration of the problem

jokes.text: Here's your stupid spy camera!
jokes.setup: Six months of Bart asking for spy camera
jokes.punchline: Frustrated mail carrier finally giving in
jokes.explanation: Payoff to the setup - shows adult frustration with kid persistence

jokes.text: Sorry, Dad. The answer to that is top secret.
jokes.setup: Homer asking what Bart is doing with the spy camera
jokes.punchline: Bart playing up the spy theme
jokes.explanation: Good kid logic - staying in character with the spy camera theme

jokes.text: Oh, sure. Like I'm really gonna take a picture of my butt.
jokes.setup: Lisa saying Bart was taking a picture of his butt
jokes.punchline: Bart's sarcastic denial while clearly having done exactly that
jokes.explanation: Classic kid denial when caught doing exactly what they're accused of

jokes.text: Only four of us? Who escaped?
jokes.setup: Marge saying it's just the four of them for dinner
jokes.punchline: Bart treating family dinner like a prison headcount
jokes.explanation: Excellent kid perspective - treating family time like imprisonment. Great economy and character voice

jokes.text: Where am I, the planet Cornball?
jokes.setup: Overly sentimental father-son speech at the bachelor party
jokes.punchline: Homer's crude assessment of the emotional moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer response to emotional sincerity - shows his discomfort with feelings through humor

jokes.text: Ahoy! I spy the children's menu.
jokes.setup: At The Rusty Barnacle restaurant with nautical theme
jokes.punchline: Bart playing along with nautical speak
jokes.explanation: Kid playing along with theme restaurant atmosphere

jokes.text: Ahoy, this place bites.
jokes.setup: Just played along with nautical theme
jokes.punchline: Immediately drops the act to complain
jokes.explanation: Great juxtaposition - maintains nautical language while expressing modern kid disdain

jokes.text: This evening I shall go for the squid platter, with extra tentacles, please.
jokes.setup: Bart ordering at a seafood restaurant
jokes.punchline: His sophisticated delivery of a gross order
jokes.explanation: Bart's mix of sophistication and grossness - trying to sound adult while ordering something disgusting

jokes.text: Hey, try to keep it down, guys. Okay? Hey, shut up.
jokes.setup: Waiter politely asking the loud party to quiet down
jokes.punchline: Immediate rude response from the party
jokes.explanation: Shows the contrast between restaurant politeness and bachelor party rudeness

jokes.text: How do I tell you this, my boy? We're in hell.
jokes.setup: Homer enjoying the bachelor party with exotic dancer
jokes.punchline: Burns' dramatic assessment of the situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns line - his prudish horror delivered with typical dramatic flair

jokes.text: Ai, caramba. Wow, man.
jokes.setup: Bart accidentally photographing Homer dancing with exotic dancer
jokes.punchline: His amazed reaction to what he's captured
jokes.explanation: Good kid reaction to stumbling onto adult situation - his catchphrase plus genuine amazement

jokes.text: The subtle graytones recall the work of Helmut Newton.
jokes.setup: Kids looking at Bart's accidental photo of Homer and dancer
jokes.punchline: Pretentious art criticism from a child
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist humor - kids treating accidental photo like high art photography

jokes.text: He brings to mind the later work of Diane Arbus.
jokes.setup: Already pretentious art criticism of Homer photo
jokes.punchline: Even more pretentious reference to famous photographer
jokes.explanation: Escalates the art criticism absurdity with another famous photographer reference

jokes.text: Cross your heart and hope to die? Stick a needle in your eye? Jam a dagger in your thigh? Eat a horse manure pie?
jokes.setup: Making kids promise not to copy the photo
jokes.punchline: Escalating rhyming promises ending with disgusting option
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of childhood oath-making, building to gross-out humor

jokes.text: Wait till I show the guys at work this little doozy.
jokes.setup: Homer just scolded Bart for having sleazy photo
jokes.punchline: Immediately wants to show it off at work
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer hypocrisy - moralizing then immediately doing the same thing. Great character consistency

jokes.text: Sorry, buddy. You got me confused with Fred Flintstone.
jokes.setup: Store clerk recognizing Homer from the photo
jokes.punchline: Homer's deflection comparing himself to another fat cartoon character
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about cartoon character resemblance, plus Homer's awareness of his appearance

jokes.text: D'oh! That purple fruit thing.
jokes.setup: Homer hoping for winning scratch-off ticket
jokes.punchline: His trademark exclamation at losing with 'purple fruit thing'
jokes.explanation: Classic D'oh moment, plus Homer's inability to identify a plum. Quintessential character moment

jokes.text: Well, a 'do dee do dee do' to you too, pint size.
jokes.setup: Someone humming at him
jokes.punchline: Homer treating humming like a greeting
jokes.explanation: Homer's literal interpretation of musical sounds as communication

jokes.text: Meaningless, Marge. Don't even attempt to find meaning in it.
jokes.setup: Marge asking about the meaning of the photo
jokes.punchline: Homer's pseudo-philosophical deflection
jokes.explanation: Homer trying to sound deep while avoiding responsibility - good character-driven deflection

jokes.text: Meaningless, Marge. Don't even attempt to find meaning in it.
jokes.setup: Marge asking about the meaning of the compromising photo
jokes.punchline: Homer's attempt to dismiss it as literally meaningless
jokes.explanation: Great Homer deflection strategy. Trying to philosophically dismiss clear evidence of his behavior is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: My suggestion is for you to sleep in the filth you created.
jokes.setup: Homer asking where he'll sleep after being kicked out
jokes.punchline: Marge's unusually harsh metaphorical response
jokes.explanation: Uncharacteristically harsh Marge delivering a memorable burn - shows how angry she is

jokes.text: What, this one? D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer complaining about photo ruining his marriage
jokes.punchline: Moe casually producing the photo, Homer's realization it's everywhere
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how widespread the photo has become, plus classic D'oh moment

jokes.text: The middle one is my house. Someone must have left the porch light on.
jokes.setup: Homer looking at distant house lights
jokes.punchline: His practical concern about electricity costs even in his emotional distress
jokes.explanation: Nice character detail. Homer's practical concerns persist even during emotional moments. Shows his middle-class anxieties.

jokes.text: The middle one is my house. Someone must have left the porch light on.
jokes.setup: Homer pointing out lights from Barney's apartment
jokes.punchline: Wishful thinking that someone left the light on vs. Marge waiting up angrily
jokes.explanation: Homer's optimistic interpretation - the light is probably Marge waiting up to yell at him

jokes.text: A plant employee carrying on like an oversexed orangutan in heat.
jokes.setup: Burns describing Homer's behavior in the photo
jokes.punchline: His overly dramatic animal comparison
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns vocabulary - unnecessarily elaborate insult that's both accurate and ridiculous

jokes.text: The fair sex remains a mystery to me.
jokes.setup: Burns revealing his loneliness and asking Homer for advice
jokes.punchline: His old-fashioned, formal way of saying he doesn't understand women
jokes.explanation: Classic Burns formal language for a universal male complaint - character voice perfect

jokes.text: You seem to have a way with women, a certain-- how shall I put it-- animal magnetisme.
jokes.setup: Burns describing his loneliness
jokes.punchline: Burns crediting Homer with 'animal magnetisme' despite just calling him an orangutan
jokes.explanation: Great callback - Burns goes from insulting Homer to envying him, plus the pretentious French pronunciation

jokes.text: You seem to have a way with women, a certain-- how shall I put it-- animal magnetisme.
jokes.setup: Burns asking Homer for romantic advice
jokes.punchline: Describing Homer's crude behavior as sophisticated 'animal magnetisme'
jokes.explanation: Great Burns character work. His misinterpretation of Homer's scandal as sophistication is perfectly delusional.

jokes.text: Well, I wine them, I... dine them, bring them flowers, write them love poetry, sir.
jokes.setup: Burns asking Homer for romantic advice
jokes.punchline: Homer giving textbook romantic advice he clearly doesn't follow
jokes.explanation: Homer giving advice that contradicts everything we know about him - classic ironic gap

jokes.text: Look, I'm not drinking out of the carton.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to show Marge he's changed
jokes.punchline: His pathetically low bar for good behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - thinking basic courtesy proves he's reformed

jokes.text: Yes, I do. Because I'm hungry, my clothes are smelly, and I'm tired.
jokes.setup: Marge saying Homer doesn't know why he's apologizing
jokes.punchline: Homer's self-centered reasons for apologizing
jokes.explanation: Great subversion - instead of understanding his wrong, Homer lists his personal discomforts

jokes.text: Princess Kashmir? You must mean April Flower. She's working over at the Girlesque.
jokes.setup: Homer looking for Princess Kashmir
jokes.punchline: Different fake exotic names for the same person at different venues
jokes.explanation: Good gag about strippers having different stage names at different venues

jokes.text: Bart! I said look at the floor.
jokes.setup: Homer taking Bart to strip clubs to find the dancer
jokes.punchline: Homer's inappropriate parenting in an inappropriate place
jokes.explanation: Cringe comedy - Homer trying to maintain parental authority in completely inappropriate setting

jokes.text: Nice to meet you, ma'am.
jokes.setup: Bart meeting exotic dancer Princess Kashmir
jokes.punchline: His polite, formal greeting to a stripper
jokes.explanation: Great juxtaposition of Bart's good manners with the inappropriate adult situation

jokes.text: My real name is Shawna Tifton. My pet peeve is rude people. And my turn-ons include silk sheets and a warm fireplace.
jokes.setup: Homer asking her to reveal her humanity to Bart
jokes.punchline: Her responses being pure centerfold clichés instead of genuine personality
jokes.explanation: Good satire of generic 'getting to know you' responses. Shows how even her attempt at humanity is commodified.

jokes.text: Get off of my stage, fat boy.
jokes.setup: Homer accidentally getting on stage during performance
jokes.punchline: Dancer's harsh but accurate assessment
jokes.explanation: Direct insult humor - works because of the situation but not particularly sophisticated

jokes.text: Get off of my stage, fat boy.
jokes.setup: Homer somehow ending up on stage during performance
jokes.punchline: The performer's blunt dismissal
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy setup. The insult is direct but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Homer Simpson, party guy.
jokes.setup: Homer being recognized from the photo
jokes.punchline: The grandiose introduction for someone who just got kicked off stage
jokes.explanation: Good ironic contrast between the hype and reality. 'Party guy' becomes an ironic title given the circumstances.

jokes.text: He's a love machine, sir.
jokes.setup: Burns asking how Homer does it with women
jokes.punchline: Smithers' assessment as Homer performs terribly on stage
jokes.explanation: Good ironic contrast between Smithers' assessment and Homer's actual performance

jokes.text: Well, not our nephews.
jokes.setup: Homer listing family relationships of women
jokes.punchline: His logical correction about nephews being male
jokes.explanation: Great mid-speech correction showing Homer's brain working in the middle of an earnest moment

jokes.text: All right, folks. Show's over. No more to see, folks. Come on. Only sick people want to see my folks kiss.
jokes.setup: Homer and Marge reconciling and kissing
jokes.punchline: Bart treating his parents' affection like a sideshow
jokes.explanation: Perfect kid reaction to parental affection - treating it like unwanted public display

tmdb_overview: Shutterbug Bart captures Homer with a sexy entertainer at a bachelor party.

guest_stars.name: Jo Ann Harris
guest_stars.character: Martin Prince / Photography Club Member #3 / Lewis (voice)

insight: Early Season 1 holds steady at 70.5 despite lacking signature standout gags.
